events.text: Andrew Nguyen, 76-year-old Asian male, Former smoker (quit 5 years ago). Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the Urinary bladder. Clinical staging: T3bN0M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C67.9. Stage III muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma of the bladder.
MRI pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 immune-related rash. Topical clobetasol 0.05% BID. Mild, no need for systemic steroids.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
MRI pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

Underwent surgical resection: Radical cystectomy with bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection and ileal conduit urinary diversion. Estimated blood loss 250 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 nephrotoxicity. Aggressive IV hydration. Creatinine monitored. Dose adjustment per CrCl.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Developed grade 2 immune-related rash. Topical clobetasol 0.05% BID. Mild, no need for systemic steroids.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 1 immune-related thyroiditis. TSH elevated to 12.5. Levothyroxine 50 mcg daily started.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.
Initiated alternative immunotherapy: Pembrolizumab 200 mg q3w. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
Developed grade 2 fatigue. Activity modification recommended. TSH and CBC checked - within normal limits.
MRI pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after alternative immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after alternative immunotherapy.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
PET/CT performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

MRI PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Andrew Nguyen
MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e
Date of Study: 2023-10-21

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: MRI performed with and without IV gadolinium contrast, multiplanar multisequence technique.

FINDINGS:
Unremarkable examination.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bladder mass with suspected extravesical extension. Muscle-invasive disease suspected.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Andrew Nguyen
MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e
Specimen ID: S2023-66505
Date of Procedure: 2023-10-23
Date of Report: 2023-10-25

SPECIMEN: Urinary bladder, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma, poorly differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.6 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Not identified
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Positive (focal)
GATA3: Positive
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive (focal)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Andrew Nguyen - Urinary bladder" is a gray-white tissue fragment measuring 4.6 x 3.2 x 2.3 cm. The cut surface reveals a soft yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A9.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a poorly differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with moderate cytoplasm and vesicular chromatin. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Andrew Nguyen
MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e
Date: 2023-10-30
Provider: Dr. Amanda Foster

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Nguyen is a 76-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder, IIIA. Patient was referred by primary care physician after abnormal imaging. Stage III muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma of the bladder. Pathology confirmed high-grade urothelial carcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports progressive dyspnea and cough x 6 weeks.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- obesity
- hyperlipidemia

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- No prior surgeries

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Never smoker
- Alcohol: Social drinker (2-3 drinks/week)
- Occupation: Retired teacher
- Lives with spouse

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Mother with breast cancer at age 62.

ALLERGIES: Iodine contrast (anaphylaxis)

MEDICATIONS:
- Atorvastatin 40 mg daily

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: constipation, abdominal bloating. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 118/69, HR 79, RR 17, Temp 98.1F, SpO2 99% on RA, Wt 72.1 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: NCAT, EOMI, PERRL. Oral mucosa moist without lesions.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear bilaterally. No rales or rhonchi.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 5.2, Hgb 10.7, Plt 109, Cr 0.87

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
76-year-old male with IIIA high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - PICC line to be placed prior to treatment
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Andrew Nguyen
MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e
Date of Report: 2023-11-02
Specimen: Urinary bladder (Biopsy)
Diagnosis: High-grade urothelial carcinoma
Panel: Tempus xT (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 16.0 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  FGFR3 p.S249C (c.746C>G) - Missense (Exon 7) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  RB1 p.R445* - Nonsense (Exon 14)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ERBB2 p.S310F - VUS
  PIK3CA p.E545A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - FGFR3 S249C: FDA-approved erdafitinib for FGFR-altered advanced urothelial carcinoma.
  - Elevated TMB may support immunotherapy benefit.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 537x coverage.
Tumor content: 68%.

Electronically signed by: Andrew Patel, MD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

FOLLOW-UP NOTE - Medical Oncology

MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Nguyen is a 76-year-old male with IIIA high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder who presents for cycle 2 assessment. Patient reports feeling well overall. Appetite has improved.

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: intermittent headaches, unintentional weight loss. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

General: Alert, oriented, well-appearing, in no acute distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally.
CV: RRR, S1/S2 normal. No murmurs, gallops, or rubs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-distended. Well-healed midline surgical scar.
Extremities: Trace bilateral lower extremity edema. Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 9.1, Hgb 14.2, Plt 296, Cr 1.06

IMAGING:
Most recent imaging reviewed.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
76-year-old male with IIIA high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. cycle 2 assessment.

1. High-grade urothelial carcinoma - cycle 2 assessment
   - Continue current treatment regimen
   - Monitoring for treatment-related toxicity
2. Symptom management
   - Pain well controlled on current regimen
3. Follow-up in 4 weeks

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Andrew Nguyen
MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e
Specimen ID: S2024-66224
Date of Procedure: 2024-03-12
Date of Report: 2024-03-14

SPECIMEN: Urinary bladder, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 5.5 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.3 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Positive (focal)
GATA3: Positive
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive (focal)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Andrew Nguyen - Urinary bladder" is a tan-white tissue fragment measuring 5.5 x 3.8 x 2.8 cm. The cut surface reveals a firm white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A11.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of nests of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and high nuclear-to-cytoplasmic ratio. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows desmoplastic reaction.

Electronically signed by: Emily Nakamura, MD
Department of Pathology

PET/CT REPORT

Patient: Andrew Nguyen
MRN: patient_7761985ebe7e
Date of Study: 2024-12-08

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. surveillance imaging.

COMPARISON: Comparison made to prior study from approximately 3 months ago.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Irregular enhancing mass along the posterior wall of the bladder measuring 3.8 cm with possible extravesical extension.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bladder mass with suspected extravesical extension. Muscle-invasive disease suspected.
2. Overall assessment: Stable disease per RECIST 1.1.

Electronically signed by: Sophia Martinez, MD
Department of Radiology

Patient: Andrew Nguyen

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. surveillance imaging.

FINDINGS:
